title: Components
description: Holds a set of reusable objects for different aspects of the OAS.

__schema1.title: Schema Object
__schema1.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema2.title: Reference Object
__schema2.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

examples.description: A user is a type of person.
A pet is a type of animal.
A pet is a type of animal.

__schema3.title: Reference
__schema3.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

__schema7.title: summary
__schema7.description: A short summary which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema8.title: description
__schema8.description: A description which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema10.title: String Object
__schema10.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema11.title: title
__schema11.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema12.title: description
__schema12.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema13.title: default
__schema13.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema14.title: example
__schema14.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema15.title: enum
__schema15.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema18.title: Discriminator
__schema18.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema19.title: propertyName
__schema19.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema20.title: mapping
__schema20.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema25.title: XML
__schema25.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema26.title: name
__schema26.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema27.title: namespace
__schema27.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema28.title: prefix
__schema28.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema29.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema29.title: attribute

__schema31.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema31.title: wrapped

__schema35.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema35.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema36.title: description
__schema36.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema37.title: url
__schema37.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema39.title: readOnly
__schema39.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema40.title: writeOnly
__schema40.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema41.title: deprecated
__schema41.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema42.title: nullable
__schema42.description: OpenAPI 3.0: Set to true to allow null values.

__schema44.title: format
__schema44.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema45.title: Pattern
__schema45.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema51.title: Number Object
__schema51.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema53.title: format
__schema53.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema54.title: multipleOf
__schema54.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema56.title: minimum
__schema56.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema58.title: maximum
__schema58.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema60.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema60.description: When true, the minimum value is exclusive (value must be > minimum).

__schema62.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema62.description: When true, the maximum value is exclusive (value must be < maximum).

__schema64.title: Integer Object
__schema64.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema66.title: format
__schema66.description: Integer size format. Use 'int32' for signed 32-bit (-2B to 2B) or 'int64' for signed 64-bit integers.

__schema67.title: multipleOf
__schema67.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema69.title: minimum
__schema69.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema71.title: maximum
__schema71.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema73.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema73.description: When true, the minimum value is exclusive (value must be > minimum).

__schema75.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema75.description: When true, the maximum value is exclusive (value must be < maximum).

__schema77.title: Boolean Object
__schema77.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema79.title: Array Object
__schema79.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema89.title: Object Object
__schema89.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema101.title: Composition Schema
__schema101.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not) without a type constraint.

__schema102.title: allOf
__schema102.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema104.title: anyOf
__schema104.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema106.title: oneOf
__schema106.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema108.title: not
__schema108.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema111.title: Response
__schema111.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema112.title: Response
__schema112.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema116.title: Header
__schema116.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema117.title: Header
__schema117.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema118.title: description
__schema118.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema119.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema119.title: required

__schema121.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema121.title: deprecated

__schema123.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema123.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema125.title: style
__schema125.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.

__schema140.title: externalValue
__schema140.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema148.title: MediaType
__schema148.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema156.title: Encoding
__schema156.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema157.title: contentType
__schema157.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema173.title: Link
__schema173.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema174.title: Link
__schema174.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema175.title: operationId
__schema175.description: The name of an existing, resolvable OAS operation, as defined with a unique operationId.

__schema176.title: operationRef
__schema176.description: A relative or absolute URI reference to an OAS operation.

__schema177.title: parameters
__schema177.description: A map representing parameters to pass to an operation as specified with operationId or identified via operationRef.

__schema180.title: requestBody
__schema180.description: A literal value or {expression} to use as a request body when calling the target operation.

__schema184.title: url
__schema184.description: A URL to the target host. Supports server variables in {braces}. May be relative.

__schema185.title: description
__schema185.description: An optional description of the server.

__schema186.title: variables
__schema186.description: A map of server variables for URL template substitution.

__schema188.title: ServerVariable
__schema188.description: A server variable for URL template substitution. Use {variableName} in the server URL.

__schema189.title: enum
__schema189.description: An enumeration of valid string values for this variable.

__schema191.title: default
__schema191.description: The default value for substitution if not supplied.

__schema192.title: description
__schema192.description: An optional description for the server variable.

__schema200.title: name
__schema200.description: The name of the query parameter. Case-sensitive.

__schema202.title: description
__schema202.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema207.description: Allows empty values (?param=). Deprecated in favor of schema-level validation.
__schema207.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema210.title: explode
__schema210.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema211.description: When true, allows reserved characters without percent-encoding.
__schema211.title: allowReserved

__schema215.title: examples
__schema215.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema217.title: content
__schema217.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema221.title: name
__schema221.description: The name of the path parameter. Must match a template expression in the path.

__schema223.title: description
__schema223.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema224.description: Path parameters must always be required.
__schema224.title: required

__schema228.title: explode
__schema228.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema231.title: examples
__schema231.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema233.title: content
__schema233.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema237.title: name
__schema237.description: The name of the header. Case-insensitive.

__schema239.title: description
__schema239.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema244.title: style
__schema244.description: Serialization style. Only 'simple' is valid for headers.

__schema245.title: explode
__schema245.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema248.title: examples
__schema248.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema250.title: content
__schema250.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema256.title: description
__schema256.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema261.title: style
__schema261.description: Serialization style. Only 'form' is valid for cookies.

__schema262.title: explode
__schema262.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema265.title: examples
__schema265.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema267.title: content
__schema267.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

additionalProperties.title: SecurityScheme
additionalProperties.description: Defines a security scheme that can be used by the operations.

__schema285.title: name
__schema285.description: The name of the header, query, or cookie parameter.

__schema286.title: in
__schema286.description: Location of the API key: 'header' (most common), 'query', or 'cookie'.

__schema287.title: description
__schema287.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema289.title: HttpSecurityScheme
__schema289.description: HTTP authentication security scheme (Basic, Bearer, etc.).

__schema292.title: bearerFormat
__schema292.description: Hint for bearer token format. Only applicable when scheme='bearer'.

__schema293.title: description
__schema293.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema297.title: flows
__schema297.description: Allows configuration of the supported OAuth Flows.

__schema299.title: OAuthFlow
__schema299.description: Configuration details for a supported OAuth Flow.

__schema300.title: authorizationUrl
__schema300.description: The authorization URL to be used for this flow.

__schema301.title: tokenUrl
__schema301.description: The token URL to be used for this flow.

__schema302.title: refreshUrl
__schema302.description: The URL to be used for obtaining refresh tokens.

__schema303.title: scopes
__schema303.description: The available scopes for the OAuth2 security scheme.

__schema316.title: openIdConnectUrl
__schema316.description: OpenID Connect URL to discover OAuth2 configuration values.

__schema322.title: Callback
__schema322.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema323.title: Callback
__schema323.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema325.title: PathItem
__schema325.description: Describes the operations available on a single path.

__schema332.title: Operation
__schema332.description: Describes a single API operation on a path.

__schema333.title: tags
__schema333.description: A list of tags for API documentation grouping.

__schema335.title: summary
__schema335.description: A short summary of the operation. Keep under ~120 characters.

__schema336.title: description
__schema336.description: A verbose description. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema337.title: operationId
__schema337.description: Unique identifier for the operation. Used for code generation.

__schema338.title: parameters
__schema338.description: Parameters for this operation, combined with path-level parameters.

__schema340.title: responses
__schema340.description: REQUIRED. The responses for this operation.

__schema344.title: deprecated
__schema344.description: Marks this operation as deprecated. Defaults to false.

__schema346.title: security
__schema346.description: Security requirements for this operation. Overrides root-level security.

items.title: SecurityRequirement
items.description: Lists the required security schemes for this operation.
